input.prompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

input.initialPrompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" ?>
<Shell
    x:Class="MyMauiApp.AppShell"
    xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
    xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
    xmlns:pages="clr-namespace:MyMauiApp.Pages"
    Title="MyMauiApp"
    FlyoutBehavior="Flyout">

    <!-- Dashboard – single page, own flyout item -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Dashboard" Icon="icon_dashboard.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Dashboard"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:DashboardPage}"
            Route="dashboard" />
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Products – flyout item with two tabs -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Products" Icon="icon_products.png">
        <Tab Title="Products">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Active"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ActiveProductsPage}"
                Route="active" />
            <ShellContent
                Title="Archived"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ArchivedProductsPage}"
                Route="archived" />
        </Tab>
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Settings – single page, own flyout item -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Settings" Icon="icon_settings.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Settings"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:SettingsPage}"
            Route="settings" />
    </FlyoutItem>

</Shell>

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to create an AppShell.xaml file for a .NET MAUI app with a specific navigation structure. Let me provide the code directly since this is a straightforward request for a code example.

Structure needed:
1. Flyout menu with:
   - "Dashboard" (single page)
   - "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and "Archived")
   - "Settings" page
2. Route registration for ProductDetailsPage

Let me create the AppShell.xaml and AppShell.xaml.cs files.

<Shell
    x:Class="MyMauiApp.AppShell"
    xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
    xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
    xmlns:pages="clr-namespace:MyMauiApp.Pages"
    Title="MyMauiApp"
    FlyoutBehavior="Flyout">

    <!-- Dashboard – single page, own flyout item -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Dashboard" Icon="icon_dashboard.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Dashboard"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:DashboardPage}"
            Route="dashboard" />
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Products – flyout item with two tabs -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Products" Icon="icon_products.png">
        <Tab Title="Products">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Active"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ActiveProductsPage}"
                Route="active" />
            <ShellContent
                Title="Archived"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ArchivedProductsPage}"
                Route="archived" />
        </Tab>
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Settings – single page, own flyout item -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Settings" Icon="icon_settings.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Settings"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:SettingsPage}"
            Route="settings" />
    </FlyoutItem>

</Shell>

data.content:
Here's the structure:

**AppShell.xaml** defines three flyout entries:

| Flyout Item | Content |
|---|---|
| Dashboard | Single `ShellContent` page |
| Products | A `Tab` with two `ShellContent` children → renders as **Active** / **Archived** tabs |
| Settings | Single `ShellContent` page |

**AppShell.xaml.cs** registers `ProductDetailsPage` as a non-visual route so you can navigate to it programmatically:

```csharp
await Shell.Current.GoToAsync("productdetails", new Dictionary<string, object>
{
    ["Product"] = selectedProduct
});
```

To receive the parameter, decorate `ProductDetailsPage` with `[QueryProperty("Product", "Product")]` or implement `IQueryAttributable`.

**Pages you still need to create** under a `Pages/` namespace:
`DashboardPage`, `ActiveProductsPage`, `ArchivedProductsPage`, `SettingsPage`, `ProductDetailsPage`.
